# Updated: 2026-05-23 17:28 GMT+8 by CLAUDE_CODE

PHASE: **SLOT 2 (incoming-call-dialog-not-rendered) CLOSED + Phase 7 realtime-engine close-out ELIGIBLE TO CONVERT PARTIAL → FULL.** This session shipped SLOT 2 as a globalThis-Symbol singleton hoist on `apps/web/src/server/socket/server.ts`. Empirical Playwright multi-context smoke (alice + bob via auth-bypass, per-frame WS instrumentation, post-click dialog count) PROVED H3 from the prior STATE.md handoff: alice's tRPC `calls.initiate` mutation returned 200 with full call payload, bob's socket connected + joined org channels (presence + in-call frames flowed correctly), bob's `myBoundDepartmentIds` returned the correct Sales dept id — yet bob received ZERO `call:incoming` WS frames in the 3.5s post-click window. Bundle forensics on `apps/web/.next/server/chunks/` revealed Next.js + webpack split `server.ts` into TWO compiled module copies: instrumentation chunk 7947 (module 67947) bound to dynamic `await import` exposes `createSocketServer:()=>l` and gets the live io; route chunk 9865 (no reference to module 67947) bound to static `import { getIO }` exposes `H:()=>d` over a module-local `ioInstance` that nothing ever assigns. Every `getIO()` from any tRPC route returned null → the `if (io !== null)` guard at calls.ts:59 silently no-op'd → `emitToOrg` dead from tRPC since Phase 7 #15 shipped 2026-05-19. Audit reach was wider than call:incoming alone. Fix: hoist `ioInstance` to `globalThis[Symbol.for("yelli.socket.io")]` so both compiled copies read/write one slot. Empirical post-fix: bob receives `42["call:incoming",{...recipientDeptId:"cmpfmyva2000fa8k1drezs7bw"...}]` at tRel=7064ms (41ms after alice's click), bob.dialog.count=1, alice navigates to `/app/call/971f919a-...`. Branch `fix/socket-io-singleton-globalthis` ready to squash-merge after governance commit. 1 NEW 🔴 [[webpack-module-duplication-singleton-trap]] joins the trap family (sibling of [[webpack-define-plugin-trap]]).
